subject: Social Science (CBSE - Social and Political Life – II)

activities_projects.title: Stereotypes Chart Activity
activities_projects.objective: Identify common gender stereotypes in society
steps: Draw two columns titled ‘Stereotypes about men’ and ‘Stereotypes about women’
Write common examples from textbook or daily life
Discuss how these stereotypes affect opportunities
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn to recognize and challenge gender stereotypes

activities_projects.title: Biography of a Change‑Maker
activities_projects.objective: Understand the contributions of women who changed the world
steps: Choose a woman (e.g., Ramabai, Rashsundari Devi, Rokeya Sakhawat Hossain) mentioned in the chapter
Research her life and key achievements
Write a short biography
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students appreciate the role of individual efforts in social change

activities_projects.title: Class Debate: Gender Roles in Work
activities_projects.objective: Discuss whether certain jobs are ‘men’s work’ or ‘women’s work’
steps: Divide class into teams ‘For’ and ‘Against’
Hold a structured debate based on chapter content
Summarize key points in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop public speaking and critical thinking

activities_projects.title: Interview Project: Women of Change
activities_projects.objective: Learn about changes in women’s opportunities over generations
steps: Interview women from different age groups in your community
Ask how opportunities for girls/women have changed
Write a summary of responses
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect textbook concepts with real experiences

activities_projects.title: Poster Making: Women in Different Professions
activities_projects.objective: Highlight the diversity of women’s work
steps: Choose at least six professions
Draw or paste pictures of women in those roles
Write short captions about each profession
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students visually explore breaking of gender roles

activities_projects.title: Role Play: Women’s Movement
activities_projects.objective: Experience key events in the women’s movement
steps: Assign students roles (activists, community members)
Perform short scenes showing campaigns or protests
Discuss why these events mattered
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students empathize with struggles for gender equality

activities_projects.title: Survey on Schoolwork at Home
activities_projects.objective: Compare recognition of work done by boys and girls
steps: Create a list of daily tasks at home
Survey classmates on who does which task
Prepare a chart to show results
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand undervalued work at home
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of data and analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Creative Writing: A Letter for Equality
activities_projects.objective: Express personal views on gender equality
steps: Imagine you are living in earlier times with fewer opportunities
Write a letter to an adult explaining why girls should get equal education
Share with class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students reflect on the value of equality

activities_projects.title: Campaign Idea Poster
activities_projects.objective: Design a campaign to promote gender equality
steps: Think of a campaign message
Design a poster encouraging equality or education for girls
Present to class with slogan
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students explore civic engagement and advocacy

activities_projects.title: Mini Quiz on Women Change the World
activities_projects.objective: Reinforce key concepts from the chapter
steps: Prepare 10 simple questions from the chapter
Ask classmates to answer them
Keep score for correct answers
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students revise and retain chapter concepts
